subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Studying Atmospheres with JWST
description: A 60-second lesson on Studying Atmospheres with JWST.

hook.text: JWST is transforming exoplanet atmosphere science. Its infrared instruments detect molecules invisible to earlier telescopes.

buildup.text: It has already detected water, carbon dioxide, and sulfur dioxide in exoplanet atmospheres with stunning clarity.

discovery.text: On WASP-39b, JWST produced the most detailed exoplanet spectrum ever—revealing photochemistry driven by its star's light.

twist.text: JWST can study smaller, cooler planets too. It's targeting rocky worlds in habitable zones for the first time.

climax.text: Early results from TRAPPIST-1 planets suggest some may lack thick atmospheres—a sobering but crucial finding.

punchline.text: Infrared eyes on alien skies. Every spectrum rewrites what we know.

quiz.question: What is JWST's advantage for studying exoplanet atmospheres?
options: Its infrared instruments detect molecules invisible to earlier telescopes
It can land probes on exoplanets to sample their atmospheres
It orbits close enough to exoplanets to photograph their surfaces
